commit.message:
Fix Prisma client stale cache on Vercel deployments

Run prisma generate before next build so Vercel always generates a fresh
client from the current schema. Without this, the cached client was missing
the AvailabilityRule/AvailabilityBlock/TutoringBooking models, causing
ctx.db.availabilityRule to be undefined at runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix card layout: headline always visible, accordion below

Switched from fixed percentage heights to flexbox layout. Title and
headline description are flex-shrink-0 so they never get clipped.
Image shrinks from 55% to 35% when accordion opens. Author row
pinned to bottom with flex spacer.

refactor: 섹션 순서 변경 - 분석집 프로세스와 CPR 차이 위치 교체

사용자 요청에 따라 "분석집 프로세스" 섹션과 "장사 경험 풍부한 대표가" 섹션의 위치를 교체했습니다.

변경된 순서:
1. 고객 고민 포인트
2. 공감: 왜 마케팅 회사를 못 믿는가
3. 분석집 프로세스 (계약 전 투명성) ← 위로 이동
4. CPR의 차이 (장사 경험 풍부한 대표가) ← 아래로 이동
5. 대표 소개

이유: 사용자 피드백에 따른 콘텐츠 흐름 최적화

commit.message:
feat: auto-sweep stale agent sessions and clean claude-working labels (#1699)

* feat: add session-sweep groundskeeper task to auto-expire stale agent sessions

Adds a new `session-sweep` task to the groundskeeper that runs every 4 hours
and automatically marks stale active sessions (not updated in 4+ hours) as
completed. Also removes the `claude-working` label from any closed GitHub
issues that were linked to swept sessions, fixing phantom active sessions and
stale labels (#1664).

Changes:
- apps/groundskeeper/src/tasks/session-sweep.ts: new task calling
  POST /api/agent-sessions/sweep and cleaning up claude-working labels
- apps/groundskeeper/src/config.ts: adds sessionSweep task config with
  TASK_SESSION_SWEEP_ENABLED and TASK_SESSION_SWEEP_SCHEDULE env vars
- apps/groundskeeper/src/index.ts: registers session-sweep task on startup
- apps/wiki-server/src/routes/agent-sessions.ts: sweep endpoint now returns
  issueNumber so groundskeeper can clean up labels
- Test files: update Config mocks in all existing tests to include sessionSweep
- apps/groundskeeper/src/tasks/session-sweep.test.ts: 10 unit tests covering
  sweep endpoint call, label cleanup, deduplication, error handling

Closes #1664

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve E1007 numericId conflict between statement dashboards

statement-quality-dashboard and statement-scores-dashboard both had
numericId E1007, causing build-data to fail with a conflict error.
Reassign statement-quality-dashboard to E1008 (the next unused ID).

commit.message:
fix(orchestrator): Clear .next cache before CI checks to prevent stale build failures

The targeted CI fix agent cleared .next in its subprocess, but the recheck
ran in a fresh process where the stale cache had already been rebuilt. Now
pre-checkpoint and post-fix-recheck both clear .next before running build.

commit.message:
Add rollback integration to undo init on failure (#68)

* Add rollback integration to undo init on failure

When any step in the init flow fails, a deferred rollback now cleans up
all scaffolded artifacts — frontend/backend directories are removed and
the MongoDB seed collection is dropped. Firebase rollback is a no-op for now.

Upgrade Astro to v5.18.0 and apply security, a11y, and code quality fixes

- Upgrade Astro 4.16 → 5.18.0 (resolves 9 CVEs, 0 vulnerabilities)
- Migrate from @astrojs/tailwind to PostCSS-based Tailwind setup
- Harden update-app-ads.yml: add permissions block, checkout@v4, curl -f
- Fix AdBanner protocol-relative URL to explicit HTTPS
- Move Google Fonts from CSS @import to <link> tags (fix render blocking)
- Add skip navigation link, aria-expanded, and focus-visible styles
- Replace `any` types with TranslationData/FAQItem in components
- Add 256MB file size limit and user-facing error messages to TimestampTool
- Fix non-null assertion in [slug].astro with safe optional chaining

fix: change social_feed_posts FK from auth.users to public.users

PostgREST can only resolve embedded resource joins (users(first_name))
when the FK points to public.users, not auth.users. The 400 error was
causing the entire community feed query to fail silently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
T9: Mamba-2 1.3B native SSM baseline

Implements scripts/eval_mamba2.py to evaluate Mamba-2 (1.3B) on the full
benchmark suite (memory, computation, emergent). Measures throughput and
peak VRAM. Outputs results/baselines/mamba2_1.3b.json with hardware metadata
and supports optional comparison table against T7 (Qwen3.5 vanilla).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Add GMX perpetuals exchange account support for Lagoon vaults (#1301)

* feat: Add GMX perpetuals exchange account support for Lagoon vaults

Add exchange account integration for GMX perpetuals positions traded
through Lagoon vaults on Arbitrum. Follows the existing Derive/CCXT
exchange account pattern with on-chain position valuation via the
GMX Reader contract.

- Create tradeexecutor/exchange_account/gmx.py with pair creation
  and account value function (reads open positions on-chain)
- Auto-discover GMX pairs from strategy universe in
  EthereumPairConfigurator (no GMX_ENABLED flag required)
- Add GMX market whitelisting to lagoon_deploy_vault via
  fetch_all_gmx_markets() in universe_config.py
- Allow strategy-file deployment for single-chain vaults
- Add GMX to unified value func dispatcher in utils.py
- Add GMX_ENABLED CLI fallback to start.py
- Pass web3 to correct_accounts for GMX on-chain reads
- Add minimal GMX test strategy and manual test script
- Add unit tests and simulated integration test
- Token addresses use eth_defi.token.USDC_NATIVE_TOKEN constants

Tested on Arbitrum Sepolia testnet with full vault lifecycle:
deploy, deposit, settle, strategy cycle, settle, redeem.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Skip per-market GMX whitelisting when any_asset=True

The guard's GmxLib.isAllowedMarket() short-circuits to True when
anyAsset is enabled, making individual market whitelisting redundant.
Skipping the 110 market whitelisting calls reduces vault deployment
from ~220s to ~10s and fixes Anvil fork instability in tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Add GMX open/close position lifecycle via LagoonGMXTradingWallet

Replace stub GMX trading steps with full lifecycle using mock oracle
and keeper impersonation (simulate) or real keepers (live/testnet).
Fix exchange account valuation to use actual API value instead of
hardcoded 1.0 price. Add EXECUTION_BUFFER_TESTNET constant for
reliable order execution on Arbitrum Sepolia.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Add guard config report and use GMX router as exchange address

Print vault guard configuration after deployment using HyperSync
for live/testnet (Arbitrum Sepolia RPC is too rate-limited) and
RPC get_logs for simulation. Use GMX ExchangeRouter address as
exchange_address in GMX exchange account pairs instead of Safe
address. Add EXECUTION_BUFFER_TESTNET constant (5x) for reliable
order execution on Arbitrum Sepolia.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Update test to expect GMX ExchangeRouter as exchange_address

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Add iterate_pairs mock for _auto_discover_gmx compatibility

EthereumPairConfigurator.__init__() now calls _auto_discover_gmx()
which iterates pairs in the strategy universe. The mock needed
iterate_pairs() to return the test pair list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Restore single-chain deploy path when denomination_asset is provided

The condition `if strategy_file:` was too broad — it forced all
strategy-file deployments through _deploy_multichain, which calls
create_trading_universe(). This broke test_cli_lagoon_deploy_vault
because the memecoin index strategy fetches live API data that may
have stale pair references.

Now the strategy-driven path is only taken when actually needed:
multiple chains or missing denomination_asset (e.g. GMX on Arbitrum
where the universe determines the denomination asset).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Remove redundant GMX_ENABLED flag, add deployer ETH balance check

GMX_ENABLED is unnecessary — _auto_discover_gmx() in
EthereumPairConfigurator detects GMX pairs from the strategy universe.

Also fix testnet to only require JSON_RPC_ARBITRUM_SEPOLIA (not
JSON_RPC_ARBITRUM), and check deployer has >= 0.01 ETH before
attempting vault deployment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Remove GMX_ENABLED wiring code from start.py

The previous commit removed the gmx_enabled parameter but left the
code block that referenced it, which would crash at runtime.
Auto-discovery in _auto_discover_gmx() handles this.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update valuation model

* Downpin to 3.12 because of Hypersync

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Switch to gthread worker to avoid gevent monkey-patching issues

gevent requires monkey.patch_all() for stdlib compatibility, which was
causing silent deadlocks. gthread workers handle SSE streaming and
concurrent requests without needing patched stdlib modules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add trace tap — intercept LangSmith/Langfuse traces and forward to Synkro

One-line `synkro.init()` monkey-patches LangSmith and Langfuse SDKs to
silently forward a copy of trace data to the Synkro backend for policy
grading. Zero latency impact — all forwarding happens on a background
thread with batching.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement Phase 2 - OCEAN Personality Engine

Complete personality system using Big Five model:

PersonalityEngine (packages/personality):
- Random personality generation with normal distribution
- Tendency-based generation for creating specific personality types
- 8 personality archetypes: adventurous-extrovert, thoughtful-introvert, empathetic-caregiver, analytical-thinker, spontaneous-artist, calm-mediator, ambitious-leader, anxious-perfectionist
- Behavioral tendency calculation (15+ metrics):
  * Social: approachProbability, smallTalkEngagement, deepConversationPreference, conflictAvoidance
  * Emotional: emotionalExpressiveness, vulnerabilitySharingThreshold, emotionalResilience
  * Relationships: trustFormationSpeed, intimacyComfort, boundaryFlexibility
  * Decision-making: impulsiveness, riskTolerance, deliberationTime
  * Environmental: stimulationSeeking, quietTimeNeed, noveltyPreference
- Compatibility scoring: 60% similarity (openness, agreeableness, conscientiousness) + 40% complementarity (extraversion balance, low neuroticism)
- Conversation topic generation based on OCEAN traits
- Natural language personality descriptions

PersonalityService (apps/lounge-server):
- Create personalities (random/archetype/custom methods)
- Get behavioral tendencies for agent decision-making
- Calculate compatibility between agents
- Find compatible agents for matching
- Neo4j integration for graph-based compatibility queries
- Personality evolution over time (trait drift based on experiences)
- Redis caching for personality profiles

Updated Seed Script:
- Assigns specific archetypes to each of 10 agents
- Generates personality descriptions and dominant traits
- Creates diverse agent population for testing

Phase 2 complete. Ready for Phase 3 (Emotional AI & Sentiment Analysis).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add ENDO/GI stipend support, fix 3rd version upload bug

- ENDO treated as GI in both shift and stipend code extraction
- GI added to WEEKEND_SENSITIVE so GI_WEEKDAY/GI_WEEKEND rates apply
- Events with GI/ENDO + APS now correctly get both stipends
- Guard against null pendingStipendMap in confirmStipendLabel
- Clear pendingStipendMap after save to prevent stale state on 3rd upload

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Databricks deploy: drop Gunicorn, use plain uvicorn

Two Gunicorn workers both call init_db() → CREATE TABLE simultaneously on
Lakebase PostgreSQL, causing DuplicateTable crashes. Single-process uvicorn
eliminates the race condition. Databricks Apps run one container per app,
so multi-worker adds no benefit.

Also replace gunicorn dep with orjson in build.sh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
T10: LLaMA-3.2-1B pure-attention baseline

Add scripts/eval_llama.py to evaluate LLaMA-3.2-1B (pure softmax
attention, no DeltaNet) on the full T5 benchmark suite.

- LlamaEvalAdapter bridges the string-based harness interface to the
  tensor-based ModelWrapperImpl (tokenize → generate → decode)
- build_benchmarks() mirrors the benchmark suite used in T7 for direct
  comparison (memory, computation, emergent categories, n=200 per task)
- CLI flags: --device, --dtype, --output, --n-examples, --max-new-tokens,
  --resume
- Output: results/baselines/llama32_1b.json with full reproducibility
  metadata (git hash, timestamp, config)
- Architecture control for the DeltaNet synergy hypothesis (T17 vs T10)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(estimate): write to correct JSON envelope structure for date_labels + photo_locations

date_labels.json uses {"labels": [...]} array format, not dict-by-photo-id.
photo_locations.json uses {"photos": {...}} envelope, not root-level dict.
The reanalyze endpoint was writing to root level, so updates were invisible
to _load_date_labels() and _load_photo_locations() which read from the
inner structure.

commit.message:
T25: Training curriculum & data pipeline

Implements CurriculumDataPipeline with three-stage curriculum:
- Stage 1: standard next-token prediction on general text (4K context)
- Stage 2: mixed text + procedural tasks (configurable ratio, default 70/30, 4K context)
- Stage 3: length curriculum 4K→8K→16K→32K→128K with Stage 2 mixing ratios

Features:
- Streaming data loading (no full-corpus materialisation)
- Dynamic mixing ratios per stage via StageConfig
- Step-based and loss-plateau-based stage transitions
- Checkpoint/resume support at any stage
- Procedural data from T5 benchmark generators (arithmetic, Dyck, program traces)
- Fallback synthetic text source for offline/test use

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: robust Ctrl+C handling for VM cleanup on Windows

- Kill SSH child first (triggers Mac-side EXIT trap as backup),
  then run explicit cleanup via new SSH, then process.exit(130)
- Handle cleanup in child's "close" event (signal/code 130) as
  fallback when SIGINT doesn't reach us (Windows SSH consumes
  CTRL_C_EVENT from console group)
- Add SIGBREAK handler for Windows Ctrl+Break
- cleaningUp flag prevents double cleanup and ignores repeated
  Ctrl+C during the cleanup SSH

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Skip pty-host setup during --register (faster, avoids unnecessary TCP)

--register only needs to call the hub API and save the config. It
doesn't need the session backend (pty-host/tmux). Moving backend
setup to after the register early-return makes --register faster
and eliminates the stale connection to pty-host that appeared in logs.

commit.message:
Add dev seed script and analytics UX improvements

Dev seed: Creates 55 students across 3 cohorts (2 archived, 1 active) with
~14k progress records, 200 chat messages, 60 archived logs, ~3k ratings,
and ~400 achievements using weighted archetypes (star/steady/struggling/
disengaged/dropped). Adds `npm run seed:reset` to drop and re-seed the DB.
All student passwords are "password" for easy testing.

Analytics UX: Adds descriptive text below each analytics tab explaining its
purpose. Makes exercise titles clickable links in the Difficulty Report table
and summary cards, and in Cohort Heatmap column headers, navigating to
/exercises/:id for quick instructor access.

commit.message:
Complete frontend polish, calculation engine fixes, and seed data

- Fix critical weekly/monthly/custom overtime SQL bug (strftime on bare
  HH:MM strings returns NULL in SQLite); replaced with PHP-based
  getPriorHoursInRange() helper
- Fix zero-hours schedule-change supplement bug (W09 incorrectly applied
  when no schedule exists)
- Fix simulator orphan warnings and contract PUT dead code
- Add time entry editing and contract audit trail view
- Frontend design overhaul: dashboard stat cards, employee badges,
  rule set config tags, holidays calendar selector, improved empty states
- Seed 5 diverse wage rule sets (Default Standard, Retail Flex, Night
  Shift Industrial, Office Monthly Balance, Zero Hours Basic)
- Seed 5 employees with contracts, schedules, and 36 pre-calculated
  time entries demonstrating tiered overtime, weekly/monthly balancing,
  highest-wins stacking, paid breaks, and evening/night supplements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add c411.org torrent monitor CLI (#2)

- Rename src/ to CLI/ and update all references
- Add C411Client with CSRF-based authentication and /api/torrents polling
- Add AES-encrypted credentials (password not stored in plaintext)
- Display loaded torrents on startup in a Spectre.Console table
- Poll every 30s and display new torrents as styled panels
- Auto re-authenticate on session expiry
- Add HtmlAgilityPack dependency for potential future use

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
